data.title: MRPeasy: The Manufacturing ERP Built for Small Factories
data.excerpt: MRPeasy gives small manufacturers full control over inventory, production, and orders. Find out why 2000+ companies swear by it. Try free today.

$media.alt: MRPeasy: The Manufacturing ERP Built for Small Factories

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through our links, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

I have a confession. For years, I thought ERP systems were reserved for Fortune 500 companies with bottomless budgets and entire IT departments on standby. Then I stumbled onto MRPeasy, and my perspective did a complete 180.

MRPeasy is a manufacturing ERP designed specifically for small and mid-sized manufacturers and distributors — companies with 10 to 200 employees who need serious production management without the enterprise-level complexity or price tag. It handles inventory, production planning, purchasing, CRM, and even accounting in one integrated platform.

If you run a small manufacturing operation and you are still juggling spreadsheets, disconnected tools, or clunky legacy software, this post is for you. Let me walk you through exactly why MRPeasy deserves your attention.
Take MRPeasy for a free spin here
Why MRPeasy Exists (And Why It Matters)

Small manufacturers face a brutal gap in the software market. On one side, you have basic tools like spreadsheets and standalone inventory apps. They work until you hit 15 orders a week, then everything starts falling apart. On the other side, you have enterprise ERPs that cost six figures to implement and require consultants to configure.
MRPeasy sits right in that middle ground — and that is exactly where thousands of small manufacturers live.

The platform was built from the ground up for companies that need real manufacturing muscle: bill of materials (BOM) management, production scheduling, lot traceability, procurement automation, and warehouse control. But it wraps all of that in an interface that a production manager can learn in a few days, not a few months.

> "I've worked in software for 15 years so I'm very picky about what we use. I am impressed with how bug-free the software is, it's much better than the industry average. MRPeasy is precisely in this middle ground."
That quote nails it. MRPeasy occupies a space that most software vendors ignore: serious manufacturing capability at a price point that does not require board approval.
If you want a deeper breakdown of the platform, check out our guide on
what MRPeasy is and how it works
I have reviewed dozens of manufacturing tools. Most of them drown you in features you will never touch. MRPeasy takes a different approach — every feature earns its place.

This is where MRPeasy shines brightest. You can plan production runs, assign work orders, and schedule operations with drag-and-drop simplicity. The system automatically calculates material requirements based on your BOMs, so you never over-order raw materials or run short mid-batch.
The drag-and-drop rescheduling feature alone saves hours every week. When a rush order comes in or a machine goes down, you can reshuffle your entire production calendar in seconds.

MRPeasy tracks stock levels across multiple warehouses in real time. It manages raw materials, work-in-progress, and finished goods separately. You get automatic reorder point alerts, so purchasing happens before you run out, not after.

Lot traceability is built in from day one. If you manufacture food, pharmaceuticals, electronics, or anything with compliance requirements, this is non-negotiable. Every component and finished product can be traced back through the entire production chain.

The system generates purchase orders automatically based on production needs and current stock. You can manage suppliers, track lead times, and compare vendor pricing without leaving the platform. For small teams that do not have a dedicated procurement department, this automation is a game-changer.

MRPeasy includes a lightweight CRM that lets you manage customer data, track sales orders, and handle quotes without bolting on another tool. It is not trying to compete with Salesforce — it is designed to keep your sales and production data in one place so nothing falls through the cracks.

Built-in accounting handles invoices, payments, and financial reporting. For companies using QuickBooks Online or Xero, MRPeasy integrates directly, syncing your manufacturing data with your accounting system seamlessly.

This is a clever feature for shop floor workers. Employees can log in via a simple web kiosk to report production progress, log time, and update task status. No fancy terminals needed — just a browser on any device.
Want practical tips on getting the most out of these features? Our
MRPeasy does not sit still. The team continuously ships updates that respond to what users actually need. Here are some highlights worth noting:

MRPeasy has introduced AI-driven capabilities into its MRP engine. The system learns from your historical data to improve demand forecasting and material planning accuracy. For small manufacturers who cannot afford a full-time demand planner, this AI layer provides real, measurable value.
The integration ecosystem keeps expanding. Current integrations include:
for connecting to hundreds of other apps
If you sell online through any major platform, orders flow directly into MRPeasy's production queue. No manual data entry, no copy-pasting between systems.

Manufacturing is global, even at the small scale. MRPeasy supports multiple languages and currencies natively, which makes it practical for companies sourcing materials internationally or selling across borders.

The reporting module has matured significantly. You get real-time dashboards for production efficiency, inventory valuation, order fulfillment rates, and more. Combined with the Power BI integration, you can build custom reports that answer the exact questions your leadership team asks.
For a complete walkthrough of the newest capabilities, visit our
Not every tool fits every business. Here is who gets the most value from MRPeasy:

This is MRPeasy's sweet spot. If you make physical products — whether that is electronics, furniture, food products, cosmetics, industrial components, textiles, or anything else — and your team falls in this range, MRPeasy was literally designed for you.
If you are currently managing production with Google Sheets, Excel, or a patchwork of disconnected apps, MRPeasy is the logical next step. It replaces all of that with a single source of truth.
MRPeasy handles distribution workflows well. If you manage warehouses, track inventory across locations, and process purchase and sales orders regularly, the platform covers those needs thoroughly.
Lot traceability, audit trails, and document management make MRPeasy suitable for industries where traceability matters — food and beverage, pharmaceuticals, aerospace, and defense, among others.
Teams That Need an All-in-One System

If you are tired of paying for five separate tools that do not talk to each other, MRPeasy consolidates production management, inventory, purchasing, CRM, and accounting into one subscription. One user said it best:
> "Our ISO auditors really liked everything that MRPeasy had. Everything is documented within the system!! And we do not need any additional systems."

Starting at $49 per user per month (Starter plan), MRPeasy delivers a feature set that rivals software costing five times as much. The Professional plan at $69 per user per month adds even more capabilities. Annual billing gets you one month free.
For real-world examples of how different industries use MRPeasy, check out our
Let me break down the numbers because I think the pricing model is genuinely fair:
Both plans allow users beyond the initial tier at $79 per 10 additional users. Annual billing gives you one month free.

For a manufacturer with 15 employees, you are looking at roughly $735 to $1,035 per month for a complete ERP system. Compare that to the cost of a single full-time hire managing spreadsheets, or the six-figure implementation of a traditional ERP, and the math speaks for itself.
Numbers tell the story better than marketing copy. Here is what MRPeasy users report:
Multiple users describe how MRPeasy's scheduling and planning tools helped them identify bottlenecks and optimize workflows, effectively doubling throughput without adding equipment or staff.
With automated procurement and real-time inventory tracking, companies reduce lead times and ship orders faster.
Less time on admin, more on growth.
> "With MRPeasy, our capacity doubled. It streamlined our production and procurement so well that I'm now able to spend a lot more time on growth."
The phrase "best value" comes up repeatedly in reviews across G2, Capterra, and Software Advice. MRPeasy consistently earns top ratings for ROI in the manufacturing software category.
MRPeasy has earned recognition for customer support quality. When a small manufacturer has a question, they need answers fast — and the MRPeasy team delivers.
Here is what the onboarding process looks like:

The learning curve is genuinely short. Most teams are operational within a week. MRPeasy provides documentation, video tutorials, and responsive support to fill any gaps.
covers everything from setup to daily operations.

If you manufacture physical products, manage inventory, or handle production scheduling, MRPeasy deserves a serious look. It delivers enterprise-grade functionality at a price that small businesses can actually afford, with an interface that does not require an IT degree to navigate.
Over 2,000 companies trust MRPeasy to run their operations. The software is stable, the support team is responsive, and the feature set grows continuously.
Start your free MRPeasy trial today
and see the difference a purpose-built manufacturing ERP makes.

children.text: What size company is MRPeasy best suited for?
MRPeasy is designed for small and mid-sized manufacturers and distributors with 10 to 200 employees. It fills the gap between basic spreadsheet-based tools and expensive enterprise ERP systems.
Does MRPeasy integrate with QuickBooks and Shopify?
Yes. MRPeasy integrates directly with QuickBooks Online, Xero, Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, Magento, ShipStation, Pipedrive, Microsoft Power BI, and Zapier, among others.

MRPeasy starts at $49 per user per month on the Starter plan and $69 per user per month on the Professional plan. Annual billing gives you one month free. Additional users beyond the initial tier are billed at $79 per 10 users.
